item.title: My feelings exactly
item.content:encoded:
Not every SCP is designed to inspire horror or lust; for the Foundation to notice an anomaly, it only needs to be inconsistent with "ordinary" reality and scientific laws. A sentient drawing enhanced by alien technology? Study it in captivity; Cassie doesn't have to be evil, spooky, or sexy to be interesting to scientists.

*grabs a sketch pad and offers Cassie a drawing of an ordinary teddy bear — friendly, innocent-faced, and very huggable*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How would 085 interact to a phone being drawn? Would the foundation be able to hear her? How about TV, or a robot companion?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would guess she could be made 3D by letting her walk onto a stereoscopical image, then viewing the image as intended. Not that it would help with anything else, though.

item.title: Re: Questions.
item.content:encoded: I guess she could transfer onto a punch card, but I seriously doubt reading the punch card into a computer (using old equipment or a replica) would do anything for her. I mean she could possibly embed a message into the punch card (if she can speak English she might speak EBCDIC), but probably not make herself into code.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given it's over 1000 in rating, unless the original author or the Rewrite Team change the text, it shall stay as is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry if this has been brought up before, but

SCP-085 prefers to be called 'Cassy.' She is completely sentient

Should this be changed to sapient? I know old articles are usually left alone, but I figured I'd ask.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well ain't this a wholesome little SCP. We need a few more of these mayhaps. Well written, nice depth to it, altogether very satisfactory article.

item.title: Plot Twist:
item.content:encoded:
You know, it's interesting how all the people who are wishing there were ways to make Cassy 3-D and not so lonely anymore are forgetting why she's so lonely. (All other drawings of animate things are still inanimate when Cassy interacts with the page's contents. And given that she's implied to have been tested with comic books, this likely applies to sentient supercomputers and robots too.)

Whose to say that if she were to be properly realized in 3D form, the same effect wouldn't happen to everything else in the 3D world? The moment Cassy makes contact with reality, everything alive and animate instantly goes limp all at once across space, potentially down to the cell given that there seem to be some sentient microbe skips. End result is that Cassy's lonely again while everything else is basically dead.

I mean, there's no canon so this could just be fear mongering, but still, it sure does sound familiar...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got a Butt-ton of dirty jokes about Cassy's whole predicament, but I also realize she's too innocent to joke about.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is she able to bring objects from one artwork to another? Also, when you say she “writes,” does this mean she’s able to write on the surface of her universe, or do you need to draw a pencil and paper into her universe for her to use them? If you drew into her reality an unremarkable-looking pencil with the informed property of being able to create objects, would she be able to start drawing objects into her reality?

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image was made by Yubi Shines, as per here. As such, it is automatically released under CC 3.0.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would it be a breach of security if SCP-085 walked over to a higher clearance document? Also I imagine another breach of containment would be having 085 walk over into a paper airplane and making it fly out of containment.
So many creative ways to, potentially, cause a breach.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Somehow, I missed this entry when I read all Series I back in the days.
Or maybe, I just don't remember it.

+1 for sure, and with mine it reaches 1000.

item.title: Questions.
item.content:encoded:
So,

If
Cassy comes in contact with a computer, could she enter it? I imagine not, since it's not paper.

What about laminated paper? Can she enter that?

Can she enter Papyrus?

And how does she perceive drawings of people? Does she see them as people, or recognize that they're not real?

Did she ever question things that didn't seem normal before she saw the documents?

Thanks for reading my questions, and if you knew the answers to any of them, that would be greatly appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, the first SCP I've felt sorry for. Nice little idea and engaging story. Poor Cassie, hopefully one day they manage to create a friend for her. Props for creating an SCP that actually makes you wistful and sad at the same time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is probably my favorite SCP, not only because I love drawing/art, but also because I want one! If I had Cassie and the living legos I would never be bored… I like this one because it inspires creativity, it leaves the reader thinking “what would I do with Cassie”. I’m impressed that I could feel so much emotion from something written (kind of?) in the style of a lab report. Good job with that! I loved the M. C. Escher part. I have recently read a book containing some of his musings and was hoping he would be in there… and then he was! I love that she can interact with other art, and using that of Escher was definitely the best choice! ;)
Props to you, SCP-085!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This shows its age a little, but Ill be damned if I didn't cry a little while reading.

+1

item.title: Re: I wonder...
item.content:encoded: I like the idea,can you make the story?

item.title: I wonder...
item.content:encoded:
About an array of paper where all the edges met in the center to form 360 degrees. Obviously the paper would need to fill any void space, requiring numerous sheets of paper. If this was done on some sort of transparent writing medium like the plastic sheets used for overhead projectors you may be able to ascertain a 3D form of the SCP. This could then potentially be transfered to another 3D medium in order to create a "real" three-dimensional representation.

I'm not roleplaying, just thinking about how this story would work out if this test were implemented.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think we should expand on this one more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing wrong with crosstesting. It wasn't laziness, especially since it was done at the beginning of the site. It was to be creative and push limits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is pretty well written. But it's the result of a cross test. More Series I laziness.

Neutral-Vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has aged surprisingly well. Reminds me of SCP-021, which I also like.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always sad our artists aren't making stuff about her. Preferably animated…

item.title: Re: So a quick question
item.content:encoded:
SCP-085 cannot transfer onto photos…

Iris creates photos. So no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, she's cute!

item.title: So a quick question
item.content:encoded: Since Cassy can move between paper, and onto pictures, could it be possible for Cassy and Iris to interact?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
She's so adorable!
It's just sad that she can't have 2D-companions.

My favorite SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EDIT: Let's try this again…

Off the top of my head there are two theories:

  • Either accidently or on purpose, she almost escaped. She may have almost died in the attempt.
  • They're worried that someone will show a little too much decency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely no paper or canvas media are allowed to exit SCP-085's containment room: any paper trash must be disposed of by incineration after careful inspection.
These requests are pending O5 review.
Requests for further diversions of this nature are pending O5-level review and approval.

What's with these ridiculously paranoid procedures and O5 involvement? I mean, this is one of the safest SCPs in containment, I don't see why we couldn't have on-site researchers make the decisions.

item.title: Re: Scientific curiosity
item.content:encoded:
There are two basic answers to the question "Has anyone tried…":

In world: Yes, but the results were obvious and boring and so aren't included in this summary.

Real world: If you can't find it, write us something interesting as a tale and everyone will rate it.

item.title: Re: Scientific curiosity
item.content:encoded:
Hm, pity. I was wondering on whether she might become a virtual artificial intelligence of some sort. She sort of already IS an artificial intelligence, only artistic/magical.

Hm, so if she were to enter a comic book or manga, would she perceive an entire frozen world of, say, Spider-Man or Naruto, or just of a single scene in each frame? If one would to draw, say, a picture of New York City from afar, would she enter an entire scaled replica, or just a bunch of buildings on distance / a bunch of medium-sized building replicas she could sit on? Would the artist have to draw detailed scenarios of every street and landmark for her to get the impression she's on a big city?

item.title: Re: Scientific curiosity
item.content:encoded:
The way I envision it, I don't think this version of Cassy would be able to extend her anomalous properties to digital copies.

Also, to what extend does she perceive a picture or comic book she walks into? Does she see a full world, third-dimensional from her perspective?

I think based on some of the article (like car driving and objects being in-motion when she interacts with them and the Escher painting), she'd perceive her world as 3D.

item.title: Scientific curiosity
item.content:encoded:
Has anyone ever tried to use a digital scanner on Cassy, make a digital copy in a computer? I wonder if the copy would also be sentient, an extension of Cassy or its own being. Would it be able to interact with the computer screen or just the picture (Note: For the sake of safety, use an offline computer with no essential information for the test)?

Also, I'm curious as to the surfaces Cassy can transfer to. Has anyone ever tried body tattoos? Also, to what extend does she perceive a picture or comic book she walks into? Does she see a full world, third-dimensional from her perspective?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is what I would kill for as a kid. I used o draw a lot as a kid and obsessed with imagination and…stuff. So, having a living drawing would be the best thing ever!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm neither staff nor the author, but one of the things I've always wanted is something animated with Cassy going through pictures and styles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh gosh, she's ADORABLE. I'd love to draw pictures for Cassy to interact with!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something has to go wrong or else a large portion of personnel are just going to end up being replaced with SCP-085 and some good artists.

See, that's the mantra - if it goes well, the writer has done something wrong. Whatever happens, it should NEVER be what the reader (or, often the AUTHOR) was expecting. Being the Foundation, things go wrong in the WORST possible way.

When everything goes well and you get what you expect… it's boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If a drawn cloning machine worked with SCP-085, and the clone retained all the knowledge of the cloned subject, well I'm too tired to think out the possibilities but you get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
although it is feasible that with enough enough training, SCP-085 could fill in for some skilled positions, assuming that SCP-085 has the aptitude.

Dude, don't put so much onto SCP-085. Is there any reason the Foundation would dump all that work onto something they aren't inherently sure they can even train for said skilled work? (also, it takes years to train 3D people to do research. Is it worth the investment?)

I'm not sure the Foundation would consider it worthwhile given that SCP-085 has yet to be damaged beyond repair. Is it worth the risk of losing the anomaly? Limited resources and all, y'know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A: Well we already know SCP-085 is capable of interacting with 2-D depictions of 3-D objects given SCP-085's exploits with the Ford Mustang. Obviously SCP-085 wouldn't be able to test ALL kinds of SCPs, those would still require the usage of foundation personnel. B: Testing of whether or not properties intrinsic to objects interacted with by SCP-085 accurately portray their real life counterparts would need to be done, although SCP-085's interactions with the car and gas station lead me to believe so. C: Unless the SCP introduced to SCP-085 is capable of inheriting SCP-085's abilities, the moment it jumps from 2-D to 3-D space it should cease to move. And assuming an SCP introduced to SCP-085 would in fact capable of jumping dimensions, it would be surprising that the ability wouldn't have manifested itself sooner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right, it wouldn't be 100% safe, but I figure it's highly unlikely that other drawn SCPs animated by SCP-085 would be capable of causing a significant amount of damage outside of their 2-D environment without ceasing to be animated.

By, "a large portion of personnel" I pretty much just mean a D-class personnel, although it is feasible that with enough enough training, SCP-085 could fill in for some skilled positions, assuming that SCP-085 has the aptitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There could be a limitless number of 2-D individuals to be used in testing all kinds of SCPs. And since the SCPs would only exist in a 2-D space it would mean 100% safe testing.

A: How would you test, say, 682, 108, or Samothrace in a 2D environment? One's 3D, another is an old Nazi bunker in a woman's nose, and the third exists only in the imagination. Maybe. B: If the answer is "have Cassie animate it", then how do we know that what Cassie animates will translate to the real world? You'll need proof, on each and every thing drawn. C: How do we know that a 2D SCP will STAY 2D? Or that it won't undergo a dramatic shift in properties upon being introduced into a 2D environment?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And since the SCPs would only exist in a 2-D space it would mean 100% safe testing.

How do we know this for sure?

a large portion of personnel are just going to end up being replaced with SCP-085 and some good artists.

SCP-085 and artists aren't trained in experimental/research design, though. And there's a ton of grunt work that needs to be done in labs. Science is not nearly as exciting as in the movies.

I tend to be really picky about these sorts of things, and I really can't see professionally-trained academics being replaced by anyone without the requisite lab/research/etc. experience. It just doesn't seem realistic.

However, if you can think it up, you should write it up. If enough people like it, it'll stay on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've only just created an account so I'm doubtful that I'll be writing up anything worthwhile in the near future, but I'll see if I can brainstorm anything up in the sandbox.

Since we already know that SCP-085 can interact with machinery, I don't see why she wouldn't be able to interact with complex machinery. And she's able to interact with fictional spaces so she should in theory be capable of interacting with fictional devices. I assume in contact means on the same page as SCP-085.

Should my ideas work as I think they would, it would bring huge changes for the SCP foundation. There could be a limitless number of 2-D individuals to be used in testing all kinds of SCPs. And since the SCPs would only exist in a 2-D space it would mean 100% safe testing.

Something has to go wrong or else a large portion of personnel are just going to end up being replaced with SCP-085 and some good artists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
• Probably not; write a story and find out.
  • "In contact" seems pretty succinct.
  • I don't think drawing a cardboard box and labeling it "Transmogrifier" would work, but you should write a story and find out.
  • I don't know; write a story and find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got some questions that I think need answering.

Would SCP-085 be able to animate artificial life?

At what point is a drawn object deemed to be in contact with SCP-085?

Couldn't someone draw a cloning machine in order to duplicate SCP-085?

Although SCP-085 can't animate animals or people, do they still retain their intrinsic qualities? For example, would a drawn Superman be impervious to damage until drawn kryptonite is introduced?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think we should totally give Cassy some more Escher objects.

We could test her to go through 'Relativity'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mixing SCPs generally causes disastrous effects that make great stories. You should get to work on that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but wonder if we were to try putting her in SCP-826. Since objects have been removed from 826 in the past, could this be a way to bring her into the real world?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't wait for you to write it into a story so we can find out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, if she draws something (like, if something drew a pencil for her and used it to draw a picture of something), is she able to interact with it, or is it only things that others have drawn? Will those pictures drawn only be in the same art type as the one she's currently in?

If the pencil has an eraser, can she erase something that someone else drew? Can she erase herself?

If she stops touching something that was moving when she was touching it (i.e. swaying trees), does she perceive it as such?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is beautiful. Literally, beautiful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome idea! SCPs like this-safe, interesting little anomalies-are some of my favorites! Well done, OP, you got my like :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it's correct. Bond refers to the type of paper and if you're curious about the process of paper manufacture, I encourage you to look it up and make a SCP about it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
chalk-white bond drawing pad

Shouldn't it be chalk-white bound drawing pad? Or am I just missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now we just need to introduce her to Fred. Really great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could use a little more testing. What happens if the paper she's on gets torn, punctured, or wet, or something is set on it? How does she interact with different types of inks (pen, pencil, marker, etc)? Would she react to an image projected onto the paper or a flashlight or laser pointer shined at it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has anyone considered cross-testing her with SCP-1230?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Slow it down. Just getting SCP-085 and SCP-423 to interact would be an experiment in itself.

SCP-423 might only be able to communicate with her as a Narrator in a comic book format, which may be no different than how she perceives outside human interactions with her.

Also SCP-423 strikes me as a shy guy. He might not be into such a big role, instead wanting to keep in the background.
